label: Moving to a New State
description: Relocating your primary residence to a different state

forms.description: Register to vote or update your voter registration in your new state
forms.deadline: Varies by state, typically 15-30 days before an election
forms.processing_time: Varies by state; typically processed within 2-4 weeks
forms.prerequisites: U.S. citizenship, meet your state's residency requirements, be 18 years old by Election Day
forms.notes: Works in 47 states (not NH, WY, or ND). Can also register at vote.gov. Some states offer same-day registration.

forms.description: Forward your mail to your new address
forms.deadline: Submit before or immediately after moving
forms.fees: $1.25 identity verification fee online; free in person at Post Office
forms.processing_time: Mail forwarding begins within 3 business days; allow up to 2 weeks
forms.prerequisites: New address, identity verification (credit card for online)
forms.notes: First-Class Mail forwarded for 12 months; periodicals for 60 days. Can also be done in person using PS Form 3575.

forms.description: Notify the IRS of your new address for tax correspondence
forms.deadline: File as soon as you move
forms.processing_time: 4-6 weeks for IRS to process
forms.prerequisites: New mailing address, SSN or ITIN, signature (both spouses if joint)
forms.notes: Use Form 8822-B if you also have a business address change. Can also update address on your next tax return.

forms.description: Update your address with the Social Security Administration
forms.deadline: Update as soon as possible after moving
forms.processing_time: Immediate when done online
forms.prerequisites: my Social Security account (Login.gov or ID.me)
forms.notes: Can be done online via my Social Security account, by phone (1-800-772-1213), or in person at a local SSA office.

forms.description: Apply for a driver's license in your new state of residence
forms.deadline: Typically 30-90 days after establishing residency, varies by state
forms.processing_time: Same-day temporary license in most states; permanent card mailed in 1-3 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Proof of identity (passport, birth certificate), proof of residency (utility bill, lease), old state driver's license, SSN, proof of lawful status (REAL ID states)
forms.notes: Requirements vary by state. Some states require written and/or driving tests for out-of-state transfers.

forms.description: Register your vehicle in your new state
forms.deadline: Typically 30-90 days after establishing residency, varies by state
forms.fees: Varies by state, typically $30-$200+ depending on vehicle value
forms.processing_time: Same-day temporary registration; permanent documents mailed in 2-6 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Current vehicle title, proof of insurance in new state, vehicle identification number (VIN), odometer reading, proof of identity and residency
forms.notes: May require vehicle inspection, emissions test, or title transfer depending on state. Sales/use tax may apply.

label: Having a Baby
description: Welcoming a new child into your family

forms.description: Register the birth and obtain official birth certificate copies
forms.deadline: Usually filed by the hospital within 10 days of birth
forms.fees: $10-$30 per certified copy, varies by state
forms.processing_time: 4-8 weeks for first certificate; additional copies available faster
forms.prerequisites: Parent identification, hospital birth registration form, mother's maiden name
forms.notes: The hospital typically initiates the process. Order multiple certified copies — you'll need them for SSN application, insurance, and other enrollments.

forms.form_name: Application for a Social Security Card (SS-5)
forms.description: Apply for a Social Security number for your newborn
forms.deadline: Apply as soon as possible after birth for tax and benefits purposes
forms.processing_time: 2-4 weeks after SSA receives the application; card mailed separately
forms.prerequisites: Child's birth certificate, parent's SSN, parent's proof of identity (driver's license, passport)
forms.notes: Can be requested at the same time as the birth certificate at the hospital (Enumeration at Birth). You need the SSN to claim the child as a dependent on taxes.

forms.form_name: IRS Form W-4 — Employee's Withholding Certificate
forms.description: Update your tax withholding to claim the new dependent
forms.deadline: Submit to employer as soon as convenient after birth
forms.processing_time: Employer implements within 1-2 pay periods
forms.prerequisites: Child's SSN (for claiming on taxes), estimated income and deductions
forms.notes: Adding a dependent may reduce your tax withholding. Submit to your employer, not the IRS. Use the IRS Tax Withholding Estimator at irs.gov.

forms.description: Enroll your newborn in health insurance or update your existing plan
forms.deadline: Within 60 days of birth (special enrollment period)
forms.fees: No enrollment fee; monthly premiums apply based on plan
forms.processing_time: Coverage can be retroactive to date of birth
forms.prerequisites: Birth certificate or hospital documentation, existing healthcare.gov account or employer HR contact
forms.notes: Birth qualifies for a 60-day Special Enrollment Period. If you have employer insurance, notify HR immediately — most employers require notification within 30 days.

forms.description: Request Family and Medical Leave for the birth of your child
forms.deadline: 30 days advance notice if foreseeable; as soon as practicable otherwise
forms.fees: Free; FMLA leave is unpaid unless employer offers paid leave
forms.processing_time: Employer must respond within 5 business days of request
forms.prerequisites: 12 months of employment with employer, 1,250+ hours worked in past 12 months, employer has 50+ employees within 75 miles, medical certification (WH-380-E form) may be required
forms.notes: FMLA provides up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave. Your employer may have their own leave request form.

label: Getting Married
description: Getting legally married and updating your records

forms.description: Apply for a marriage license in the county where you plan to marry
forms.deadline: Apply before the wedding; licenses are valid for a limited period (varies by state)
forms.fees: $30–$100 depending on county; some states offer discounts for premarital counseling
forms.processing_time: Same-day to 5 business days; some states have a 1–3 day waiting period after issuance
forms.prerequisites: Government-issued photo ID for both parties, Social Security numbers, divorce decree if previously married, birth certificates (some counties)
forms.notes: Requirements vary by county — check local clerk's office for fees, waiting periods, and required documents

forms.form_name: Application for a Social Security Card (SS-5) — Name Change
forms.description: Update your name on your Social Security card after marriage
forms.deadline: No strict deadline, but do this before updating other documents
forms.processing_time: 2–4 weeks for new card; SSA updates records within 24–48 hours
forms.prerequisites: Certified marriage certificate (original or certified copy), current valid ID (driver's license or passport), completed SS-5 form
forms.notes: Required before you can update your name with the DMV, passport office, or employer. Bring certified marriage certificate.

forms.description: Update the name on your driver's license or state ID
forms.deadline: Varies by state, typically within 30-60 days of name change
forms.fees: $10–$40 depending on state; some states charge standard renewal fee
forms.processing_time: Same-day temporary license; permanent card mailed in 1–3 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Updated Social Security card (or SSA confirmation letter), certified marriage certificate, current driver's license, proof of residency (some states)
forms.notes: Update your SSA records first. Bring marriage certificate, updated Social Security card, and current license.

forms.description: Update the name on your U.S. passport after marriage
forms.deadline: No deadline, but update before international travel
forms.fees: Free if passport issued within 1 year (DS-5504); $130 book / $30 card if older (DS-82 renewal)
forms.processing_time: 6–8 weeks routine; 2–3 weeks expedited ($60 extra)
forms.prerequisites: Current passport, certified marriage certificate, updated SS card recommended, passport photo, completed DS-5504 or DS-82 form
forms.notes: If passport was issued less than 1 year ago, use Form DS-5504 (free). Otherwise, use DS-82 (renewal with name change).

forms.form_name: IRS Form W-4 — Update Filing Status
forms.description: Update your tax withholding to reflect married filing status
forms.deadline: Submit to employer; new status applies for the full tax year of marriage
forms.processing_time: Employer processes within 1–2 pay periods
forms.prerequisites: New spouse's SSN (for filing jointly calculations), completed W-4 form
forms.notes: Marriage may change your optimal withholding. Consider using the IRS Tax Withholding Estimator.

label: Death of a Family Member
description: Handling affairs after the death of a close family member

forms.description: Obtain certified copies of the death certificate
forms.deadline: Order as soon as possible; you'll need multiple copies for various agencies
forms.fees: $10–$30 per certified copy depending on state; order 10–15 copies
forms.processing_time: 1–2 weeks for certified copies; funeral home files initial certificate within 3–10 days
forms.prerequisites: Deceased's full legal name, SSN, date/place of birth, date/place of death, next of kin information, funeral home information
forms.notes: The funeral home usually helps file the death certificate. Order at least 10 certified copies.

forms.description: Report the death to the Social Security Administration
forms.deadline: Report as soon as possible
forms.fees: Free; a one-time $255 lump-sum death benefit may be available to surviving spouse or child
forms.processing_time: SSA processes within 1–2 weeks; survivor benefits may take 2–4 weeks to begin
forms.prerequisites: Deceased's SSN, certified death certificate, proof of relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate), your own SSN and ID
forms.notes: The funeral home may report the death, but you should confirm. Surviving spouse/dependents may be eligible for survivor benefits.

forms.description: File the deceased person's final federal income tax return
forms.deadline: April 15 of the year following the death (standard tax deadline)
forms.fees: Free to file; tax preparation costs vary
forms.processing_time: Standard IRS processing: 3–6 weeks for e-filed, 6–8 weeks for paper
forms.prerequisites: Deceased's W-2s/1099s, prior year tax returns, SSN, certified death certificate, Letters Testamentary or court appointment as executor
forms.notes: Write 'DECEASED' across the top of the return with the date of death. The surviving spouse or executor files this.

forms.form_name: IRS Form 56 — Notice Concerning Fiduciary Relationship
forms.description: Notify the IRS that you are acting as executor or administrator of the estate
forms.deadline: File as soon as you are appointed as fiduciary
forms.processing_time: 4–6 weeks for IRS acknowledgment
forms.prerequisites: Letters Testamentary or court order appointing you as fiduciary, deceased's SSN and EIN (if estate has one), your SSN and contact information
forms.notes: Required if you are the personal representative handling the deceased person's tax matters

forms.description: File the will and open probate proceedings if required
forms.deadline: Varies by state; typically within 30-90 days of death
forms.fees: $50–$400+ depending on state and estate value; attorney fees additional
forms.processing_time: 6 months to 2+ years depending on estate complexity and state; simple estates may take 3–6 months
forms.prerequisites: Original will (if one exists), certified death certificate, petition for probate form (varies by county), list of assets and heirs
forms.notes: Not all estates require probate. Small estates may qualify for simplified procedures. Consult an attorney.

label: Starting a Small Business
description: Launching a new business or becoming self-employed

forms.form_name: IRS Form SS-4 — Application for Employer Identification Number
forms.description: Apply for an EIN (Employer Identification Number) for your business
forms.deadline: Before hiring employees, opening a business bank account, or filing business taxes
forms.processing_time: Immediate if applied online; 4–5 weeks by mail; 4 business days by fax
forms.prerequisites: Your SSN or ITIN (responsible party), legal name of business, business address, type of entity (LLC, corp, sole prop, etc.)
forms.notes: Online application is the fastest — you receive your EIN immediately. Available Mon-Fri, 7am-10pm ET.

forms.description: Register your business entity (LLC, Corporation, etc.) with your state
forms.deadline: Before conducting business in the state
forms.fees: $50–$500 depending on state and entity type; LLC typically $50–$200, Corporation $100–$300
forms.processing_time: 1–5 business days online; 2–4 weeks by mail; expedited processing available in most states for extra fee
forms.prerequisites: Business name (check availability first), registered agent name and address, Articles of Organization (LLC) or Articles of Incorporation, member/officer information
forms.notes: Requirements and fees vary by state and business type. The SBA has links to each state's registration portal.

forms.description: Register a 'Doing Business As' name if operating under a name different from your legal name
forms.deadline: Before using the business name publicly
forms.fees: $10–$100 depending on county/state; some require newspaper publication ($40–$200 extra)
forms.processing_time: Same-day to 2 weeks; newspaper publication requirement adds 4–6 weeks in some states
forms.prerequisites: Legal name of business owner or entity, desired DBA name, business address, proof of entity registration (if LLC/Corp)
forms.notes: Required if your business name differs from your legal name (sole proprietor) or registered entity name

forms.description: Register to collect and remit sales tax if selling taxable goods or services
forms.deadline: Before making your first taxable sale
forms.fees: Free in most states; some states charge $0–$25 registration fee
forms.processing_time: Immediate to 2 weeks depending on state; online applications typically processed within 1–3 business days
forms.prerequisites: EIN or SSN, state business registration, business address, estimated monthly sales volume, NAICS code / description of goods sold
forms.notes: Not all states have sales tax (e.g., Oregon, Montana, Delaware, New Hampshire, Alaska). Check your state's requirements.

forms.description: Obtain a general business license or permit to operate in your locality
forms.deadline: Before commencing business operations
forms.fees: $25–$500+ depending on city, business type, and industry; annual renewal fees may apply
forms.processing_time: 1–4 weeks; zoning approval or inspections may add additional time
forms.prerequisites: EIN, state business registration, proof of zoning compliance, lease agreement or proof of business location, industry-specific certifications if applicable
forms.notes: Requirements vary widely by location and industry. Use the SBA license and permit tool to find what you need.

label: Buying a House
description: Purchasing a home or residential property

forms.description: Apply for a property tax exemption on your primary residence
forms.deadline: Varies by state/county; often within 1 year of purchase or by a specific annual deadline
forms.processing_time: 4–8 weeks; exemption typically applies to the next tax year
forms.prerequisites: Recorded deed or closing documents, proof of residency (driver's license with property address), property tax ID / parcel number
forms.notes: Not all states offer homestead exemptions. Check your county's property appraiser website for the specific form and deadline.

forms.description: Forward your mail from your old address to your new home
forms.deadline: Submit before or immediately after moving
forms.fees: $1.25 identity verification fee for online submission; free by mail
forms.processing_time: 7–10 business days for forwarding to begin; temporary forwarding lasts 12 months
forms.prerequisites: Old address, new address, valid credit or debit card for identity verification (online), valid ID (in-person)
forms.notes: Online submission has a $1.25 identity verification fee

forms.description: Notify the IRS of your new home address
forms.deadline: File as soon as you move
forms.processing_time: 4–6 weeks for IRS to process the address change
forms.prerequisites: Your SSN, old address, new address, signature (and spouse's signature if filing jointly)
forms.notes: Ensures you receive tax correspondence at your new address

forms.description: Set up electricity, gas, water, and other utility accounts at your new address
forms.deadline: Schedule before move-in date
forms.fees: Connection fees vary; $0–$100 per utility; deposits may be required ($50–$200)
forms.processing_time: 1–5 business days for new service activation; schedule at least 2 weeks before move-in
forms.prerequisites: New home address, move-in date, SSN (for credit check), government-issued ID, previous utility account info for transfer
forms.notes: Contact each utility provider directly. May require a deposit.

forms.description: Obtain homeowners insurance coverage (typically required by mortgage lender)
forms.deadline: Must be in place before closing
forms.fees: Annual premium varies: national average ~$1,800/year; paid at closing or monthly via escrow
forms.processing_time: Quote in 1–2 days; binding coverage same-day to 1 week; proof of insurance (binder) needed before closing
forms.prerequisites: Property address and details (sq ft, year built, roof age), closing date, mortgage lender info, personal info for underwriting
forms.notes: Your mortgage lender will require proof of insurance before closing. Shop multiple providers for best rates.

label: Getting Divorced
description: Going through a divorce and updating your legal and financial records

forms.description: File the initial petition to begin divorce proceedings
forms.deadline: No federal deadline; residency requirements vary by state
forms.processing_time: Uncontested: 30-90 days; contested: 6-18+ months
forms.prerequisites: Marriage certificate, proof of residency, government-issued ID, completed state-specific petition form
forms.notes: Divorce is handled at the state court level. Each state has its own forms and procedures. Most states require residency for 30 days to 1 year before filing.

forms.description: File a proposed parenting plan or custody agreement
forms.deadline: Typically filed with or shortly after the divorce petition
forms.fees: Typically included with divorce filing fee; standalone petition $50-$300
forms.processing_time: 30 days to 12+ months depending on whether contested
forms.prerequisites: Divorce petition or separate custody filing, children's birth certificates, proof of residency, proposed parenting schedule
forms.notes: Required if the divorce involves minor children. Courts prioritize the best interests of the child. Each state provides free fillable forms.

forms.form_name: Application for a Social Security Card (SS-5) — Name Change
forms.description: Revert to your previous name if desired after divorce
forms.deadline: No deadline; complete after divorce decree is finalized
forms.processing_time: Typically 2 weeks from receipt
forms.prerequisites: Completed Form SS-5, divorce decree or court order showing name change, proof of identity (unexpired U.S. driver's license, state ID, or passport)
forms.notes: Must be completed before updating name with DMV or passport office. Limit of 3 name-change cards per year, 10 per lifetime. Some name changes can be done online at ssa.gov/myaccount.

forms.form_name: IRS Form W-4 — Update Filing Status
forms.description: Update your tax withholding to reflect your new filing status
forms.deadline: Update with employer within 10 days of marital status change
forms.processing_time: Employer implements within 1-2 pay periods
forms.prerequisites: Updated marital/filing status, estimated income and deductions
forms.notes: Submit to your employer, not to the IRS. Filing status changes from 'Married Filing Jointly' to 'Single' or 'Head of Household'. Use IRS Tax Withholding Estimator at irs.gov to determine correct withholding.

forms.description: Update your address with the IRS if you've moved
forms.deadline: File as soon as you move to a new address
forms.processing_time: 4-6 weeks for IRS to process
forms.prerequisites: New mailing address, SSN or ITIN, signature (both spouses if previously filed jointly)
forms.notes: Important if you previously filed jointly and now need IRS correspondence at your individual address. Can also update address on your next tax return.

label: Retiring
description: Transitioning from working life to retirement

forms.description: Apply for Social Security retirement benefits
forms.deadline: Apply up to 4 months before you want benefits to start. Earliest eligibility is age 62.
forms.processing_time: A few weeks when applied online
forms.prerequisites: Social Security number, birth certificate or proof of birth, U.S. citizenship or lawful status proof, DD-214 if applicable, W-2 or self-employment tax returns for last year, bank account info for direct deposit
forms.notes: Apply online at ssa.gov/apply. Full retirement age is 66-67 depending on birth year. Benefits reduced ~30% if claimed at 62 vs. full retirement age. Can also apply by calling 1-800-772-1213.

forms.description: Enroll in Medicare health insurance coverage
forms.deadline: Initial Enrollment Period: 7-month window around your 65th birthday. General Enrollment: Jan 1 - Mar 31 each year.
forms.fees: Part A: Free for most (10+ years of Medicare taxes). Part B: ~$185/month (2025, adjusted by income).
forms.processing_time: Enrollment processed through SSA, typically a few weeks
forms.prerequisites: U.S. citizen or permanent legal resident for 5+ continuous years, age 65+ (or under 65 with qualifying disability), Social Security number
forms.notes: You sign up through Social Security, not Medicare directly. IMPORTANT: Missing your Initial Enrollment Period results in a 10% Part B premium increase per 12-month delay, lasting for life. General Enrollment Period coverage starts July 1.

forms.form_name: CMS Form 40B — Application for Enrollment in Medicare Part B
forms.description: Enroll in Medicare Part B (medical insurance) during a Special Enrollment Period
forms.deadline: Within 8 months of losing employer coverage
forms.fees: No filing fee; Part B monthly premium applies (approx. $185/month in 2025, adjusted annually by income)
forms.processing_time: Processed by SSA, typically a few weeks
forms.prerequisites: Eligible for Medicare Part A, within an enrollment period (Initial, General, or Special), employer coverage proof (CMS-L564) if applying during SEP
forms.notes: Use this form if you delayed Part B enrollment because you had employer coverage. Also need Form CMS-L564 (Request for Employment Information) signed by your employer. Late enrollment penalty: 10% premium increase per 12-month period delayed.

forms.form_name: IRS Form W-4P / W-4R — Withholding from Pensions and Annuities
forms.description: Set up federal tax withholding on your pension or retirement distributions
forms.deadline: Submit to your pension plan administrator before or when distributions begin
forms.processing_time: Payer implements within 1-2 payment cycles
forms.prerequisites: Pension/annuity or deferred compensation distribution, estimated total income for the year
forms.notes: Submit to payer, not the IRS. W-4P is for periodic (recurring) pension/annuity payments. W-4R is for one-time/nonperiodic distributions. Default withholding is 10% for nonperiodic, 20% for eligible rollover distributions.

forms.description: Request distributions from your employer-sponsored retirement plan
forms.deadline: Required Minimum Distributions must begin by April 1 of the year after turning 73
forms.fees: No government fee; plan administrators may charge processing fees. Early withdrawal penalty of 10% if under age 59.5 (with exceptions).
forms.processing_time: Typically 3-10 business days after plan administrator processes request
forms.prerequisites: Qualifying distribution event (retirement, separation from service, age 59.5+, or hardship), completed distribution form from your specific plan administrator
forms.notes: Distribution forms are plan-specific (Fidelity, Vanguard, Schwab, etc.). Contact your plan administrator directly. Consider tax implications and rollover options. The DOL/EBSA provides regulatory oversight.

label: Immigration / Green Card
description: Applying for permanent residence (green card) in the United States

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-130 — Petition for Alien Relative
forms.description: Petition filed by a U.S. citizen or permanent resident to establish a family relationship
forms.deadline: No deadline, but processing times vary. File as early as possible.
forms.processing_time: Immediate relatives: 5-15 months; family preference categories: years due to visa backlogs
forms.prerequisites: Petitioner must be U.S. citizen or LPR, proof of petitioner's status, proof of qualifying relationship (marriage/birth certificate), passport-style photos
forms.notes: Can be filed online through myUSCIS. Separate I-130 required for each relative. Check uscis.gov/feeschedule for current fees.

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-485 — Application to Register Permanent Residence
forms.description: Apply for a green card (adjustment of status) from within the United States
forms.deadline: Can be filed concurrently with I-130 for immediate relatives of U.S. citizens
forms.processing_time: 8-25+ months depending on category and field office
forms.prerequisites: Approved immigrant petition (I-130/I-140), immigrant visa immediately available, Form I-693 (medical exam), Form I-864 (Affidavit of Support), passport photos, birth certificate with English translation, evidence of legal U.S. entry
forms.notes: Must submit I-693 with I-485. Can concurrently file I-765 (work permit) and I-131 (travel document).

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-765 — Application for Employment Authorization
forms.description: Apply for a work permit (Employment Authorization Document / EAD)
forms.deadline: Can be filed concurrently with I-485
forms.processing_time: 3-7 months; auto-extensions available for some renewal cases
forms.prerequisites: Pending I-485 or other qualifying status, passport-style photos, government-issued ID
forms.notes: Online filing available. Allows you to work in the U.S. while your green card application is pending. Use eligibility category (c)(9) for AOS applicants.

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-131 — Application for Travel Document
forms.description: Apply for Advance Parole to travel outside the U.S. while your green card is pending
forms.deadline: Can be filed concurrently with I-485
forms.processing_time: 3-6 months; expedite may be available for urgent travel
forms.prerequisites: Pending I-485 or other qualifying status, passport-style photos, travel itinerary or explanation
forms.notes: CRITICAL: Leaving the U.S. without approved Advance Parole while I-485 is pending may result in abandonment of your application. USCIS may issue a combo card (EAD + Advance Parole).

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-693 — Report of Medical Examination and Vaccination Record
forms.description: Complete the required immigration medical examination
forms.deadline: Must be submitted with I-485. Valid for 2 years from civil surgeon's signature.
forms.fees: No USCIS fee; civil surgeon exam costs $200-$500+ (not covered by most insurance)
forms.processing_time: Civil surgeon exam: 1-2 visits
forms.prerequisites: Appointment with USCIS-designated civil surgeon, vaccination records, photo ID
forms.notes: The applicant does NOT fill this out — it must be completed by a USCIS-designated civil surgeon. Find one at https://my.uscis.gov/findadoctor. Required vaccinations include MMR, Varicella, Tdap, Influenza, COVID-19.

forms.form_name: USCIS Form I-864 — Affidavit of Support
forms.description: The petitioning sponsor demonstrates financial ability to support the immigrant
forms.deadline: Required at time of I-485 filing or interview
forms.processing_time: Reviewed as part of I-485 adjudication
forms.prerequisites: Sponsor must be U.S. citizen or LPR age 18+, domiciled in the U.S., federal tax returns for 3 most recent years, proof of income (pay stubs, W-2, employment letter)
forms.notes: Legally binding contract — sponsor agrees to maintain immigrant at 125% of Federal Poverty Guidelines (~$25,550/year for household of 2). Obligation lasts until immigrant becomes U.S. citizen or earns 40 qualifying quarters of work.

label: Child Starting School
description: Enrolling your child in school for the first time

forms.description: Register your child for school in your district
forms.deadline: Varies by district; kindergarten registration typically opens March-May for the following fall
forms.fees: Free — public school enrollment is free
forms.processing_time: Typically 1-5 business days
forms.prerequisites: Child's birth certificate or proof of age, proof of residency, immunization records, previous school records (if transferring), parent/guardian ID
forms.notes: All children have the right to enroll in public school regardless of immigration status. Contact your local school district for forms and requirements.

forms.description: Obtain official immunization records required for school enrollment
forms.deadline: Required at time of school enrollment; some states allow a 30-day grace period
forms.fees: Free from most state registries; pediatrician offices may charge $10-$25
forms.processing_time: Immediate to a few days for state registry lookups
forms.prerequisites: Child's immunization history from pediatrician, access to state immunization registry
forms.notes: All 50 states require vaccinations for school entry; specific requirements and exemptions vary. All states allow medical exemptions; some allow religious or philosophical exemptions.

forms.form_name: USDA Free and Reduced Price School Meals Application
forms.agency: U.S. Department of Agriculture / School District
forms.description: Apply for free or reduced-price breakfast and lunch for your child
forms.deadline: Can be submitted at any time during the school year; new application needed each school year
forms.processing_time: Schools must process within 10 working days of receipt
forms.prerequisites: Household income information, number of household members, child's name and school, SSN of adult household member (or indicate no SSN)
forms.notes: Free meals at 130% or below Federal Poverty Guidelines; reduced-price at 185% or below. Children receiving SNAP, TANF, or FDPIR are automatically eligible. Many districts now offer free meals to all students via Community Eligibility Provision.

forms.description: Provide documentation proving you reside within the school district boundaries
forms.deadline: Required at time of enrollment; some districts require annual verification
forms.fees: Free; notarization may cost $5-$15 if an affidavit is needed
forms.processing_time: Verified at time of enrollment
forms.prerequisites: Utility bill in parent/guardian name, lease or mortgage statement, property tax bill, or government-issued ID with current address
forms.notes: Requirements set by each school district. The McKinney-Vento Act protects homeless students' enrollment rights even without traditional residency documentation.

forms.description: Provide a recent physical exam form signed by your child's doctor
forms.deadline: Required before the first day of school or within a grace period
forms.fees: Covered by most insurance under preventive care; without insurance $100-$300
forms.processing_time: Completed at the physician visit; forms available same day
forms.prerequisites: Appointment with licensed healthcare provider, child's immunization records, insurance card if applicable
forms.notes: The physical exam form is state-specific — contact your school district or state Department of Education for the required form. Schedule the exam well in advance.

label: Selling Your Car
description: Selling a personal vehicle requires proper title transfer, registration updates, and tax reporting to complete the transaction legally.

forms.description: Transfers legal ownership of the vehicle from seller to buyer by signing over the title certificate
forms.deadline: at time of sale
forms.processing_time: Immediate when done properly
forms.prerequisites: Original vehicle title, valid ID, odometer reading
forms.notes: Both buyer and seller must sign; incomplete transfers can leave seller liable for future violations

forms.form_name: Notice of Transfer and Release of Liability
forms.description: Notifies the DMV that you have sold the vehicle and releases you from liability for future tickets or accidents
forms.deadline: within 5-30 days of sale (varies by state)
forms.processing_time: 1-2 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Completed sale, buyer information, sale date and price
forms.notes: Critical for liability protection; some states call this a 'bill of sale' filing

forms.description: Cancels your vehicle registration and may provide partial refund of unused registration fees
forms.deadline: within 30 days of sale
forms.processing_time: 2-4 weeks for refund processing
forms.prerequisites: Proof of sale, current registration certificate
forms.notes: Return license plates in states that require it; keep documentation for refund claims

forms.description: Cancels auto insurance coverage for the sold vehicle to avoid unnecessary premium payments
forms.deadline: as soon as possible after sale
forms.processing_time: Immediate
forms.prerequisites: Policy number, sale date, proof of sale
forms.notes: May receive partial premium refund; maintain continuous coverage if you have other vehicles

forms.description: Reports capital gains or losses from the vehicle sale if sold for more or significantly less than basis
forms.deadline: by April 15th of following tax year
forms.processing_time: Part of annual tax return
forms.prerequisites: Original purchase price, sale price, records of improvements
forms.notes: Usually no tax impact for personal vehicles sold at a loss; consult tax professional for valuable cars

forms.description: Creates written record of the transaction with sale price, vehicle details, and both parties' information
forms.deadline: at time of sale
forms.processing_time: Immediate
forms.prerequisites: Vehicle identification number, buyer and seller information
forms.notes: Required by law in many states; protects both parties and may be needed for tax purposes

forms.description: Obtains clear title by paying off any outstanding loan and getting lien release from lender
forms.deadline: before sale can be completed
forms.processing_time: 1-3 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Loan payoff, lender cooperation
forms.notes: Cannot legally sell vehicle with active lien without lender involvement; some states allow simultaneous payoff at sale

label: Educational Expedition to Antarctica with Students
description: Organizing and leading a group of high school students on an educational expedition to Antarctica.

forms.description: Required passenger and crew manifest information for international travel
forms.deadline: 72 hours before departure
forms.processing_time: Immediate
forms.prerequisites: Valid passports for all participants
forms.notes: Must be submitted by airline or charter operator

forms.description: Required environmental review for all Antarctic activities under the Antarctic Conservation Act
forms.deadline: 6-12 months before travel
forms.processing_time: 3-6 months
forms.prerequisites: Detailed expedition plan and itinerary
forms.notes: Required under the Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty

forms.description: Valid U.S. passport required for international travel for each student and chaperone
forms.deadline: 6-8 weeks before travel (expedited available)
forms.processing_time: 6-8 weeks standard, 2-3 weeks expedited
forms.prerequisites: Birth certificate, photo ID, passport photos
forms.notes: Minors require parental consent forms

forms.description: Notarized parental consent for minor students traveling internationally
forms.deadline: 30 days before travel
forms.processing_time: Same day with proper documentation
forms.prerequisites: Parent/guardian identification and student information
forms.notes: May require both parents' signatures for divorced parents

forms.agency: International Association of Antarctica Tour Operators
forms.description: Verification that tour operator has proper Antarctic Treaty compliance and insurance
forms.deadline: Before booking expedition
forms.processing_time: Immediate verification
forms.prerequisites: Tour operator name and registration number
forms.notes: Ensure operator is IAATO member for legitimate Antarctic access

forms.description: Register group travel with nearest U.S. Embassy for emergency assistance
forms.deadline: Before departure
forms.processing_time: Immediate
forms.prerequisites: Passport numbers and travel itinerary for all participants
forms.notes: Critical for emergency evacuation assistance in remote locations

forms.agency: Private Insurance Provider (as required by school district)
forms.description: Comprehensive travel insurance including medical evacuation coverage for Antarctic travel
forms.deadline: Before departure
forms.processing_time: 1-5 business days
forms.prerequisites: Participant information and travel dates
forms.notes: Medical evacuation from Antarctica can cost $100,000+ - comprehensive coverage essential

forms.description: Medical examination and clearance for each participant for extreme environment travel
forms.deadline: 30-60 days before travel
forms.processing_time: 1-2 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Physical examination and medical history review
forms.notes: May require specialist clearance for students with pre-existing conditions

forms.description: Official school district approval for international educational field trip
forms.deadline: 3-6 months before travel
forms.processing_time: 2-8 weeks
forms.prerequisites: Educational objectives, safety plan, and budget approval
forms.notes: May require school board approval for high-risk international travel
